canonical_definition: Dividing a graph into disjoint subgraphs such that nodes within each subgraph are strongly connected internally while minimizing connections between subgraphs, under size or balance constraints.

definitions.text: Dividing a graph into disjoint subgraphs such that nodes within each subgraph are strongly connected internally while minimizing connections between subgraphs, under size or balance constraints.

relationships.evidence: To validate our approach, we construct multi-scale graph partitioning datasets based on Cora and conduct comprehensive experiments. (Section 5.1)

To further assess the scalability and practical utility of our method, we also experimented with three larger real-world graphs that exceed the direct processing capacity of vanilla LLMs such as Qwen2.5-Plus, Qwen2.5-Max, and DeepSeek-R1: ... and FB15k-237... (Section 5.1)

characterization: Dividing a graph into disjoint subgraphs such that nodes within each subgraph are strongly connected internally while minimizing connections between subgraphs, under size or balance constraints.
